title: Taguchi Quality Loss Function Calculator
description: Calculates the financial loss due to deviation from target specification using Taguchi's quadratic loss function, incorporating cost of poor quality, process capability, and hidden factory losses. Aligns with Six Sigma and Lean methodologies.

inputs.label: Target Value (T)
inputs.businessContext: The ideal nominal specification for the product characteristic (e.g., length, weight, voltage).

businessContext_i18n.en: The ideal nominal specification for the product characteristic (e.g., length, weight, voltage).

inputs.label: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)
inputs.businessContext: Minimum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.

businessContext_i18n.en: Minimum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.

inputs.label: Upper Specification Limit (USL)
inputs.businessContext: Maximum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum acceptable value per engineering or customer requirement.

inputs.label: Process Mean (μ)
inputs.businessContext: Current average of the process output.

businessContext_i18n.en: Current average of the process output.

inputs.label: Cost at Specification Limit (A₀)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated financial loss (rework, scrap, warranty) when a unit is exactly at the specification limit.

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated financial loss (rework, scrap, warranty) when a unit is exactly at the specification limit.

inputs.label: Deviation at Limit (Δ)
inputs.businessContext: Distance from target to the nearest specification limit (Δ = min(T-LSL, USL-T)).

businessContext_i18n.en: Distance from target to the nearest specification limit (Δ = min(T-LSL, USL-T)).

inputs.label: Production Volume (N)
inputs.businessContext: Total number of units produced in the period.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total number of units produced in the period.

rules.message: Lower specification limit must be less than upper specification limit.
Target value must be within specification limits.
Process mean must be within specification limits for meaningful loss calculation.
Standard deviation must be greater than zero.
Deviation at limit must be greater than zero.
Cost at limit must be greater than zero.
Inspection rate must be between 0 and 100.

thresholds.message: Loss per unit exceeds $10. Consider process improvement.
Process capability (Cpk) below 1.33. Immediate action recommended.
Total quality loss exceeds $50,000. Escalate to management.
Hidden factory losses exceed 30% of total loss. Investigate rework and scrap.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
